old_claim.note: Mass Media (Newspapers) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Newspapers supplied the subscriber market and distribution channel for wire services; early agencies gathered and sold news to newspaper clients.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica's history section describes large newspapers relying on agencies, early Havas/AP/Reuters/Wolff agency formation, and provincial newspaper subscriptions to telegraphed agency text.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes newspapers as the client and distribution base for early news agencies.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That relationship supports newspapers as an enabling market/distribution infrastructure for wire-service agencies rather than a loose background medium.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: transoceanic_news_agencies remains enabled by mass_media_newspapers, now with subscriber-market evidence.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to non-newspaper private intelligence services only.
A stronger source showed early transoceanic wire agencies did not sell to or depend on newspapers.
The mass_media_newspapers node were narrowed away from daily newspapers as a subscriber medium.
short_reason: Early wire agencies sold telegraphed news to newspapers.
